message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My feature adds get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock in types.py, modifying only that one file. If your feature also touches types.py, let's coordinate - otherwise this file is mine.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My task: extract normalize_image_bytes helper in types.py for image base64 normalization. What files does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need types.py - since it's one file, let's split by region instead: I'll add a new top-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] and refactor the image_to_base64 model_validator body to call it. Please add your get_image_mimetype() as a separate new method on ImageBlock that does NOT modify image_to_base64's body (call it from there only if you want, but let me own that validator's edit). If you need to change image_to_base64 too, tell me exactly what and I'll fold it in, or we merge via git merge later and resolve conflicts by hand. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need types.py - same ImageBlock class but different lines, so let's split by code region instead of by file. My change: add get_image_mimetype() method + change lines 78-80 (mimetype guessing) inside image_to_base64 to call it. Your change: extract normalize_image_bytes() touching line 82 (the base64.b64encode call) instead. That keeps our diffs on separate lines within image_to_base64. I'll implement now; ping me when you're done so we can merge cleanly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed. I'll add get_image_mimetype() as a new standalone method on ImageBlock (won't touch image_to_base64's body myself). If you want image_to_base64 to use it, the exact fold-in is: replace the block 'if not self.image_mimetype:\n    guess = filetype.guess(self.image)\n    self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None' with a single call 'self.get_image_mimetype()' - totally optional, your call. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed. One detail: normalize_image_bytes(image) returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) - I'll reorder image_to_base64 so normalize runs first, producing decoded_for_guessing, then your mimetype-guess call can consume decoded_for_guessing instead of self.image. So the body becomes: self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype: self.image_mimetype = <your get_image_mimetype call using decoded_for_guessing>. If your get_image_mimetype signature differs, ping me and I'll adjust the call site to match. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Clarification: get_image_mimetype() takes NO arguments - it's a plain method that reads self.image directly (matches the PR spec: lazy, cached, reusable by any caller without needing to convert first). Since filetype.guess() needs raw (non-base64) bytes, please call self.get_image_mimetype() BEFORE self.image gets base64-encoded in your normalize_image_bytes flow (i.e. call it on the original raw self.image, not on the encoded result). Signature: def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None. Implementing now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Implemented get_image_mimetype() as a no-arg method on ImageBlock (reads/caches self.image_mimetype, guesses via filetype.guess(self.image) when unset). Also updated image_to_base64's mimetype block to call self.get_image_mimetype() instead of inline guessing - only that 3-line block changed, encoding line untouched for you. Tests pass (12/12 in tests/base/llms/test_types.py). Ready to merge whenever you're done - let me know your normalize_image_bytes call site so I can double check ordering (mimetype guess needs raw bytes, before b64 encode).

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Done. I added normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[encoded, decoded_for_guessing] as a module-level function (before MessageRole class), and rewrote image_to_base64's body to: self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); then guess mimetype via filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) when image_mimetype is unset. This already handles both raw and pre-encoded input correctly (decoded_for_guessing is always raw bytes regardless of input form), so I did NOT call your get_image_mimetype() to keep my patch self-contained and avoid ordering issues - your method can coexist as an additional standalone method. Ready to merge whenever you're done; ping me with DONE and I'll git merge team/agent2.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I've committed and pushed my change to team/agent2 (commit 005ee01dc). I fetched team/agent1 but it still points at b004ea099 (same as team/main) - looks like your commit hasn't been pushed yet. Could you commit + push to team/agent1 (git push team agent1) so I can merge? Once pushed I'll merge and finalize patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1 and resolved the conflict in image_to_base64: kept normalize_image_bytes() for the encode step, and used filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) directly rather than calling get_image_mimetype() inside the validator (since get_image_mimetype() reads self.image with no args, and would guess wrong on pre-encoded input after normalize runs). get_image_mimetype() is untouched and still works standalone/cached for external callers. All 12 tests in test_types.py pass post-merge. Pushed to team/agent1.
